{{Quote|He is really throwing his weight around on the track!|[[Dax Gazaway]], a race announcer|<ref name="RR">''[[Star Wars: Racer Revenge]]''</ref>}}
The son of the [[Devlikk/Legends|Devlikk]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Podracing/Legends|Podracing]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Wan Sandage/Legends|Wan Sandage]], '''Wan Sandage Jr.''' picked up his [[Parent/Legends|father's]] fallen reigns after the elder's [[Death/Legends|death]]. Flying his father's old [[Elsinore-Cordova Engineering]] [[TurboDyne 99-U/Legends|''TurboDyne'' 99-U]] [[Podracer/Legends|Podracer]], Sandage participated in the Podracing season of [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]], joining in the [[Podracing Open]] and [[Hutt Championships]] circuits. Even though he was sometimes confused for his father, Sandage nonetheless went out to make a name for himself, gaining popularity on a race course located on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]] known as [[Serres Sarrano (track)|Serres Sarrano]]. He went on to win various course records for best single lap and most knockouts during a single race on the planets of [[Gamorr/Legends|Gamorr]], [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]], and [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. Sandage continued to race through [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], where the sport had opened up courses on the planets [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and [[Felucia/Legends|Felucia]].

==Biography==
===Like father, like son===
{{Quote|Watch the wreckage and debris in here. This'll be tight!|[[Dax Gazaway]], describing the Serres Sarrano race course|<ref name="RR" />}}
The [[Gender/Legends|son]]<ref name="RRG" /> of the [[Devlikk/Legends|Devlikk]] playboy<ref name="DB">{{DB|character|wansandage|Sandage, Wan}}</ref> and [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracer]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Wan Sandage/Legends|Wan Sandage]], Wan Sandage Jr. took up the [[Podracing/Legends|Podracing]] lifestyle of his [[Parent/Legends|father]] after the elder [[Death/Legends|expired]].<ref name="RRG" /> Piloting the very same [[Elsinore-Cordova Engineering]] [[TurboDyne 99-U/Legends|''TurboDyne'' 99-U]] [[Podracer/Legends|Podracer]] that his father had, Sandage joined the Podracing season<ref name="RR" /> of [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]],<ref name="RR2">''[[Star Wars: Racer Revenge]]'' takes place eight [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']], according to the game's opening scrawl. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of ''The Phantom Menace'' to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], thereby providing a date of [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]] for ''Racer Revenge''.</ref> entering the intermediate [[Podracing Open]] circuit and the more advanced [[Hutt Championships]], despite being an amateur.<ref name="RRG" />

New to the sport, Sandage set out to quickly prove himself. However, too often did he find himself engaging in deadly scrapes and rough conflicts with other racers on the courses. Yet his aggressive play and eagerness to win earned him some praise on the<ref name="RRG" /> [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]<ref name="Geo">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''</ref> [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]], where Sandage was promoted to the stature of track favorite for the first event of the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] Championships, a [[Serres Sarrano (track)|course]] that wove through the [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Serres Sarrano]]. Despite his popularity on the track, many Podracing fans did not realize that Sandage was not his father. Only to the keen [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] of hardcore Podracing fans was the difference between father and son recognizable.<ref name="RRG" />

===On the Podracing circuit===
{{Quote|Wan Sandage takes the lead!|[[Fodesinbeed Annodue/Legends|Fode Annodue]]|<ref name="KSW">''[[Kinect Star Wars]]''</ref>}}
[[File:SandageJr_Redux.png|thumb|left|263px|Following in his father's footsteps, Wan Sandage Jr. became a professional Podracer.]]
Throughout the Podracing Open, Sandage found himself rising through the statistics. His aggressive play became evident on the planet [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]], where, for [[The Brightlands]] racecourse, Sandage obtained the highest overall record for vehicle knockouts during a single race, standing with four total knockouts. His piloting abilities, on the other hand, were demonstrated on the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] planet of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], where he scored the second top [[Time/Legends|time]], 01:13.92 [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]], for a single lap on the track known as the [[Badlands (Tatooine)/Legends|Badlands]]. For the [[Ballast Complex]] course on [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]], another world hosting some of the season's Podracing courses, Sandage scored the third top single lap time for the course, standing at 01:17.03 minutes. The final course of the Podracing Open was on the jungle planet of [[Gamorr/Legends|Gamorr]], on a twisted racetrack known as the [[Ruins of Carnuss Gorgull/Legends|Ruins of Carnuss Gorgull]]. The track favorite was the aggressive [[Ghishi (species)|Ghishi]] pilot [[Kraid Nemmeso]], who topped the knockout chart for the track with a total of five. Sandage stood on the chart right below Nemmeso, with a total of three knockouts. Moving into the Hutt Championships, the Devlikk held no records for any of the courses, including the Serres Sarrano course, where he was most popular.<ref name="RRG" />

At some point, Sandage raced on a [[Unidentified Podracing city course|track]] that sped through a [[City/Legends|city]]. During the race, Sandage found himself trapped between the Podracers Nemmeso and the [[Glymphid/Legends|Glymphid]] [[Aldar Beedo/Legends|Aldar Beedo]]. When the two pilots tried to crush Sandage, the resourceful Devlikk pulled hard on his brakes, causing the two other pilots to ram into each other and destroy themselves, allowing Sandage to continue unharmed.<ref name="RR" /> Sandage later competed in the year [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="KSW1">In ''[[Kinect Star Wars]]'', the [[Podracing/Legends|Podrace]] announcers state that the Podracing segment takes place ten [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the events of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates ''The Phantom Menace'' to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], thereby providing a date of [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] for the ''Kinect Star Wars'' Podracing segment.</ref> going up against many competitors, including Aldar Beedo,<ref name="KSW" /> who had once worked for Wan Sandage Sr.,<ref name="DB" /> the [[Nosaurian/Legends|Nosaurian]] pilot [[Clegg Holdfast/Legends|Clegg Holdfast]], and the [[Vulptereen/Legends|Vulptereen]] pilot [[Dud Bolt/Legends|Dud Bolt]]. The season visited regular course locations such as Tatooine, but also expanded into worlds such as [[Felucia/Legends|Felucia]], [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], [[Utapau/Legends|Utapau]], and [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="KSW" />

==Personality and traits==
Wan Sandage Jr. was a thrill seeker like his father and followed in the elder Sandage's footsteps to mount up as a daring Podracer pilot. However, the young Devlikk was almost too eager to prove himself on the courses and constantly found himself in dangerous scrapes with other pilots. Even so, Sandage eventually took control of his piloting and managed to turn his knack for danger into an aggressive tendency that found himself making it onto the knockout charts.<ref name="RRG" />

Sandage bore a very close resemblance to his father, with a very similar shade of [[Color/Legends|tan]] [[Hair/Legends|fur]] and the same green plumage that ringed his neck and topped his head. Like his father, Sandage tied up the hair that hung from his cheeks with small bands and beads. Sandage had orange eyes and long, lanky [[Limb/Legends|arms]] that ended in sharp claws. He also had light-colored skin, along with thin, red lips.<ref name="RRG" /><ref name="DB" />

[[File:TurboDyne99-U-CT.png|thumb|right|200px|Wan Sandage Jr.'s ''TurboDyne''-99 was the same Podracer as his father's.]]

==Skills and abilities==
As a pilot, Wan Sandage Jr. showed notable skill on the Podracing courses, racking up both speed and knockout records during his career, thanks to his keen reflexes and agility, which allowed Sandage to successfully traverse the hazardous obstacles and terrain that professional Podracing courses often presented.<ref name="RR" /> Sandage was also one to involve himself with in-race scrapes but was able to escape any lasting damage.<ref name="RRG" />

==Equipment==
Wan Sandage Jr. flew the very same Elsinore-Cordova Engineering ''TurboDyne'' 99-U Podracer that his father had, its appearance unchanged. The ''TurboDyne'' was still well renowned for excellent traction, and by the time it was in the young Sandage's clawed hands, the vehicle overall had a well-balanced set of specs, including a sturdy exterior and plenty of room for upgrades with regards to its cooling system. The Podracer had good acceleration, a high top speed, and good handling, which allowed Sandage the best in control when at said top speeds. As during the elder Sandage's [[Standard day/Legends|days]], the Podracer was still painted in a light-beige color across the [[Engine/Legends|engines]] and the [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]]. The engines' insides were also adorned with green and yellow markings, along with yellow lettering on the wings of the cockpit.<ref name="RRG" />

Although the Devlikk did not use any [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]] or gear for the 24 BBY season,<ref name="RRG" /><ref name="RR2" /> he later adopted a full piloting [[uniform]], which included [[Goggles/Legends|goggles]] to protect his eyes and a neck-brace. His neck was also protected by a brown colored garment that wrapped around his entire neck. He also took to wearing a shirt that matched the color of his neck garment. He also wore white pants, and took to wearing a [[Belt/Legends|belt]].<ref name="KSW" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:KinectSandage.jpg|thumb|right|125px|Wan Sandage ''Kinect Star Wars'' concept art]]
Wan Sandage Jr. first appeared in the [[2002]] [[Wikipedia:PlayStation 2|PlayStation 2]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Racer Revenge]]'' as a playable character. In the game, the character is simply referred to as Wan Sandage.<ref name="RR" /> The game's [[Star Wars: Racer Revenge: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]] revealed that he was actually the son of the character who appeared in [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']], naming him as Wan Sandage Jr.<ref name="RRG" /> Sandage is voiced in the game by [[Gregg Berger]], who also provided the voice for [[Tzidik Wrantojo]] in ''Racer Revenge''.<ref name="RR" /> Berger also provided the voice for the elder Wan Sandage in the [[1999]] video game [[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'']].<ref name="e1r">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'']]</ref>

A Podracer named Wan Sandage also appeared in the [[2012]] [[Wikipedia:Xbox 360|Xbox 360]] video game ''[[Kinect Star Wars]]'', appearing as a computer-controlled opponent during Podraces. However, as the Podracing segment takes place in 22 BBY,<ref name="KSW1" /> this Wan Sandage cannot be the elder Sandage, as he had died beforehand.<ref name="RRG" /> This article assumes that the Sandage Jr. and the ''Kinect'' Sandage are the same character. Concept art for the ''Kinect'' character was created by [[Robert St.Aubin]].<ref name="KSW" /> [[The Star Wars rough draft|Early]] [[The Star Wars first draft|drafts]] of the original [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'']] film contained a character named [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Sandage]]. The character was omitted from later drafts, but the name was later reused for the Podracer Wan Sandage. As such, the name and history behind it was passed down to Wan Sandage Jr.<ref name="DB" />
